title: RAF Typhoon jet shoots down Iranian drone heading to Qatar

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: On Sunday evening, a Ministry of Defence spokesperson confirmed the incident had taken place during a “defensive air patrol”. They said: “On 1 March 2026, a Royal Air Force Typhoon operating from Qatar as part of the joint UK–Qatar Typhoon Squadron, successfully took out an Iranian drone heading towards Qatari territory. “The Typhoon jet was conducting a defensive air patrol and used an air‑to‑air missile to shoot down the drone, ensuring the security of Qatar’s airspace and British interests in the region. Our Armed Forces are playing a vital role to protect our people, our interests and our Allies.”

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: Thousands of British nationals, mainly in the UAE, have already registered their presence with the Foreign Office. The BBC reports that a squadron of Typhoons were deployed to Qatar earlier this year as tensions with Iran rose. On Saturday, Prime Minister Keir Starmer said that RAF planes were “in the sky” for defensive purposes. The incursion on Sunday afternoon is the first instance of a UK fighter plane shooting down an Iranian drone during the current conflict.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: Minister of State for the Armed Forces Al Carns reacted to the news on X, writing: “The professionalism of our RAF is remarkable. Good work, keep it going.”

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – RAF Typhoon shoots down Iranian drone heading towards Qatari airspace
